comments.author: @normanazeez1816
comments.text: So many new streets have been built. Every way you go, there's tremendous improvement. Why don't stupid people causing mistrust, when around the world you see the state of India, China Philippines, Indonesia, Vietnam under water, an entire city and villages. But just a few drops of rain falls and people blaming the government. Y'all should be proud you have a place to put your head at night, and a job to go to. Shame on you who spreads untrue tales. God sees and hears everything. SHAME SHAME SHAME.😮😮😮😮😮

comments.author: @phillipdaguiar_01
comments.text:
Don't waste your time with fakes and lies from the demented and uneducated, intended for making easy money on YouTube. 
..................................................................................................................................................................................................
There are genuine up to date Videos / Documentaries of Guyana on YouTube. 
So don't be a victim of YouTube "Scammers" and "Fakers".
You may want to look at  night views by drones of Georgetown and the newly commissioned world class bridge .
Every country got crimes, and so is Guyana, but gross exaggeration is "Sick Minded" and fear mongering.
And try to photograph certain buildings or "hang around" in the USA in certain 'Garbs" with a backpack shall most possibly be taken as an "Enemy
Combatant" and may never see "Daylight" again.
..................................................................................................................................................................................................
How about free University education for all, including Post Grad. and thousands of additional Gov't paid scholarships offered to Guyanese to study at Universities from UK, US and Canada.
Police officers and Military are facilitated to study degree programs including Law at the University of Guyana free. 
Recently, student loans been "wiped off".
Free world class Health Care from any of 12 brand new hospitals with most up to date equipment and technology money can buy,  as Guyana is partnered with two of US largest Providers.
Telemedicine in implementation.
A 1.5 Billion USD 300 Megawatt  GTE Plant to cut electrical energy to consumers and Businesses costs in half by end of year, also the most kilowatt hours per capita in South America. Also, a second Plant under consideration for future demands. There are also Hydro-Electric Dams to be added and at present large solar farms in remote areas.
Guyana is the only country in the world that is self sufficient in food production and export to many countries (See Google AI)
Thousands of subsidised houses built each month.  
Every citizen 18 yrs. and over gets 500 USD cash grants twice yearly.
Every school child gets a 500 USD cash grant yearly for their Uniforms and all school supplies, including laptops are free.
Parents of each newborn gets a cash  grant.
Every school teacher gets to buy a duty-free new car.
Interest free loans and grants are available for start up businesses.
The world's first "Smart City" is under construction.
The most digitized country in South America.
Guyana is accepting University Qualified Professionals in most Disciplines, as Immigrants from chosen countries.
A country that is Democratic, as per English and Western values and a Member of the Commonwealth Group of Countries.
Recently served as a Member on the UN Security Council.
The Venezuela's claim is bogus and been settled since 1899 and was Maduro's "Hail Mary". We know how that went!
Guyana is God's Country and shall remain for the benefit of Humanity, the birds, fishes and animals. 
One of the most beautiful unspoiled country on the planet.
No need to worry, will be ok. 
Guyanese are smart and a resilient people, well known in many countries.
What's there not to love about Guyana?

comments.text: Whenever you've blacks ruling themselves, it's always a mess, it's 2926 yet we don't have one developed black nations, it's like corruption and incompetency is in the race .

comments.author: @GlobalCountryExplorer
comments.text: I’ve watched many world documentaries, but Guyana’s story amazed me. The contrast between new oil wealth, old challenges, and resilient people shows a country at a historic turning point.

comments.author: @GlobalDailyLife
comments.text: I'm interested to learn more about how rapid development can affect a country's infrastructure and daily life. Which part of this story surprised you the most while researching it?
